DATuM
  • transformations
  • View page source

transformations

transformations.py

This module defines the transformations.CoordinateTransformer class, which works in conjunction with the preprocessor.Preprocessor class to provide essential methods for transforming the Beverli stereo PIV data from the local PIV coordinate system to the global BeVERLI coordinate system.

Methods:

get_transformation_parameters(): Obtain coordinate transformation parameters.

datum.transformations.get_rotation_matrix(rotation_angle_deg, rotation_axis)

Compute rotation matrix for Beverli stereo PIV data, i.e., for a rotation about the [0, 0, 1] axis.

Parameters:

  • rotation_angle_deg (Real) – Rotation angle in degrees.

  • rotation_axis (Tuple[float, float, float]) – Axis of rotation.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

Rotation matrix as NumPy ndarray of shape (3, 3).

datum.transformations.calculate_unit_vector(data)
Parameters:

data (Tuple[float, float, float]) –

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

datum.transformations.rotate_data(piv_obj)

Perform a comprehensive routine to rotate all stereo PIV data.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.rotate_flow_quantity(piv_obj, data_type, keys, rotation_matrix)

Rotate a desired quantity of the Beverli stereo PIV data. :type piv_obj: :param piv_obj: Insert description :type data_type: str :param data_type: String specifying the quantity to rotate. :type keys: List[str] :param keys: A list specifying the components of the vector or tensor

quantity to rotate.

Parameters:

rotation_matrix (ndarray) – The rotation matrix used to rotate the data as NumPy ndarray of shape (3, 3).

Return type:

None

Note: The rotated data is directly saved to Piv.data.

datum.transformations.rotate_vector_quantity(rotation_matrix, components)

Rotate a stereo PIV vector quantity based on the provided rotation matrix.

This method takes a rotation matrix and a list of vector component matrices as input. It then calculates the rotated quantity and returns it.

Parameters:

  • rotation_matrix (ndarray) – NumPy ndarray of shape (3, 3) representing the rotation matrix.

  • components (List[ndarray]) – List of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), each containing a specific vector component.

Return type:

List[ndarray]

Returns:

List of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n) containing the rotated vector quantity’s components.

datum.transformations.rotate_tensor_quantity(rotation_matrix, components)

Rotate a stereo PIV tensor quantity based on the provided rotation matrix.

This method takes a rotation matrix and a list of tensor component matrices as input. It then calculates the rotated quantity and returns it.

Parameters:

  • rotation_matrix (ndarray) – NumPy ndarray of shape (3, 3) representing the rotation matrix.

  • components (List[List[ndarray]]) – List of lists of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), each containing a specific vector component.

Return type:

List[List[ndarray]]

Returns:

List of lists of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n) containing the rotated vector quantity’s components.

datum.transformations.prepare_vector_quantity_for_rotation(piv_obj, data_type, keys)

Build the vector of a desired quantity from the corresponding components of the stereo PIV data.

Parameters:

  • data_type (str) – String specifying the vector quantity.

  • keys (List[str]) – List of strings specifying the vector components.

Return type:

List[ndarray]

Returns:

List of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), each containing a specific vector component.

datum.transformations.prepare_tensor_quantity_for_rotation(piv_obj, data_type, keys)

Build the tensor of a desired quantity from the corresponding components of the stereo PIV data.

Parameters:

  • data_type (str) – String specifying the tensor quantity.

  • keys (List[str]) – List of strings specifying the tensor components.

Return type:

List[List[ndarray]]

Returns:

List of lists of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), containing specific tensor components.

datum.transformations.set_rotated_vector_quantity(piv_obj, data_type, keys, rotated_vector)

Update the PIV data with the rotated vector.

Parameters:

  • data_type (str) – String specifying the vector quantity.

  • keys (List[str]) – List of strings specifying the vector components.

  • rotated_vector (List[ndarray]) – List of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), each representing a vector component.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.set_rotated_tensor_quantity(piv_obj, data_type, keys, rotated_tensor)

Update the PIV data with the rotated tensor.

Parameters:

  • data_type (str) – String specifying the tensor quantity.

  • keys (List[str]) – List of strings specifying the tensor components.

  • rotated_tensor (List[List[ndarray]]) – List of lists of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n). Each array represents a tensor component.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.interpolate_data(piv_obj)

Perform a comprehensive routine to interpolate the stereo PIV data to a dense grid within the global BeVERLI coordinate system.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.get_interpolation_grid(piv_obj)

Build a structured, dense coordinate mesh for the interpolation of the BeVERLI stereo PIV data.

Return type:

Tuple[ndarray, ndarray]

Returns:

A tuple containing NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n) that represent a mesh grid.

datum.transformations.get_flattened_coordinates(piv_obj, data_type)

Retrieve an array of flattened spatial coordinates from the stereo PIV data, necessary for subsequent interpolation.

Parameters:

data_type (str) – A string specifying the desired quantity to interpolate.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

NumPy ndarray of shape (m, 2) containing the flattened set of spatial coordinates for the desired quantity from the stereo PIV data.

datum.transformations.get_flattened_data(piv_obj, data_type, key)

Retrieve an array of flattened data of a specific vector or tensor quantity from the stereo PIV data, necessary for subsequent interpolation.

Parameters:

  • data_type (str) – A string specifying the desired quantity to interpolate.

  • key (str) – A string indicating the desired vector or tensor component.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

NumPy ndarrays of shape (n, ) containing the flattened set of values for the desired quantity’s component from the stereo PIV data.

datum.transformations.set_interpolated_data(piv_obj, data_type, key, interpolated_data)

Update the PIV data with the interpolated data.

Parameters:

  • data_type (str) – A string specifying the particular vector or tensor quantity to update.

  • key (str) – A string specifying the particular component of the vector or tensor quantity to update.

  • interpolated_data (ndarray) – NumPy ndarrays of shape (n, ) representing the interpolated component.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.translate_data(piv_obj)

Perform a comprehensive routine to translate the stereo PIV data from its local to the global BeVERLI coordinate system.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.get_translation_vector(piv_obj)
datum.transformations.translate_coordinates(coordinates, x_1_shift, x_2_shift)

Perform the coordinate translation from the local PIV to the global Beverli coordinate system.

Parameters:

  • coordinates (Dict[str, ndarray]) – Dictionary containing the stereo PIV coordinates.

  • x_1_shift (Union[float, int]) – Coordinate shift in the x_1 direction.

  • x_2_shift (Union[float, int]) – Coordinate shift in the x_2 direction.

Return type:

None

Note: This method is laid out without return value, such that the PIV data can be directly updated with the translated data by passing the PIV data container Piv.data directly to as an argument for the coords parameter.
